channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Xtreme Gaming, Parivision, and NAVI Miss Out on PGL Wallachia Season 7 Playoffs
item.content:encoded:
PGL Wallachia Season 7’s playoffs are underway after the conclusion of the Swiss Stage, which saw Xtreme Gaming, Parivision, and NAVI fall narrowly short of the playoffs.

Vici Gaming, BetBoom, and Tundra joined Liquid, Spirit, Heroic, Aurora, and Yandex in the playoffs for the second PGL Wallachia of the 2025/26 DotA 2 season.

Disappointing end for fan-favourite orgs

TI 2025 runners-up, Xtreme Gaming, fell to regional rivals Vici Gaming. VG, while a legendary organisation in DotA 2, only returned to the DotA circuit in October following its departure in 2023.

The regional victory, combined with wins over OG and Falcons, shows that Vici may be a serious contender already despite their short time since returning as they look to recapture their historic success with Guo ‘Xm’ Hongcheng.

The 2-3 elimination was a disappointing follow-up for NAVI after their BLAST Dota Slam VI runners-up placement, which saw them top the Round Robin standings, in a run that included wins against MOUZ, Falcons, and Tundra, before eventually falling to Liquid in the Grand Final.

While NAVI did once again take Tundra down at Wallachia, but their run was brutally ended by BetBoom, who swept NAVI aside in two 33-minute contests.

Parivision’s mediocre start to the year continued with their elimination to Tundra. The Russian team still seems to be finding its feet following the departure of coach Filipe ‘Astini’ Astini Ribeiro and the installation of Clement ‘Puppey’ Ivanov in his place.

Wins against Yellow Submarine and NAVI were followed by the nightmare scenario of three losses in a row against Spirit, Yandex, and Tundra in a hard-fought series that averaged over 50 minutes per game.

PGL Wallachia Season 7 Playoffs are already underway with BetBoom taking down Heroic 2-0 to send them to the lower bracket. Elsewhere, VG takes on Team Spirit, Aurora plays Tandex, and Tundra will face BLAST DotA Slam VI winners, Team Liquid.

Who do you think will take home the title at PGL Wallachia Season 7? Discuss that and more on Insider Gaming’s Discord!

For more Valve-related news, see the latest on the lawsuits filed against the Valve Corporation.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/pgl-wallachia-season-7-playoffs/

item.title: Today’s Contexto #1271: Hints & Answer For March 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, March 12, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: A form of snow
  • Hint 2: Small and flat
  • Hint 3: Popular cereal, Corn ______

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Crumb
  • Pellet
  • Salt
  • Pepper
  • Crush
  • Bran
  • Powder
  • Dry
  • Granule

Today’s Contexto Answer For Game #1271 On March 12, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Flake

A Flake is a small, flat piece of something, typically one which has broken away from a larger piece.

When Is The Next Contexto Game After #1271?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on March 13.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more word game guides, check out the following:

  • NYT Connections

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Counter-Strike 2 Update Brings New Skins and Gloves As Lawsuits Loom
item.content:encoded:
Counter-Strike 2’s new update brought 17 new weapon skins and 22 new glove finishes to the game in a new collection entitled the ‘Dead Hand Collection.’

The update reflects a significant shift in Valve’s philosophy on its skin economy, as the game continues to move away from cases as the primary way to acquire skins.

All Dead Hand Collection skins:

  • AWP | Queen’s Gambit – Covert
  • Glock-18 | Fully Tuned – Covert
  • P90 | Deathgaze – Classified
  • P250 | Kintsugi – Classified
  • AK-47 | Crane Flight – Classified
  • MP9 | Urban Sovereign – Restricted
  • Galil | AR Galigator – Restricted
  • MP7 | Amberline – Restricted
  • M4A1-S | Electrum – Restricted
  • Desert Eagle | Firebreathing – Restricted
  • PP Bizon | RMX – Mil-spec
  • USP-S | Silent Shot – Mil-spec
  • Five-Seven | Dark Polymer – Mil-pec
  • Sawed-Off | Fusion – Mil-spec
  • UMP-45 | Fragment – Mil-spec
  • M4A4 | Zubastick – Mil-spec
  • M249 | Bock Blocks – Mil-spec

Dead Hand Collection Gloves:

  • Driver Gloves | Brocade Crane
  • Driver Gloves | Brocade Flowers
  • Driver Gloves | Dragon Fists
  • Driver Gloves | Garden
  • Driver Gloves | Hand Sweaters
  • Driver Gloves | Plum Quill
  • Driver Gloves | Seigaiha
  • Driver Gloves | Wave Chaster
  • Specialist Gloves | Big Swell
  • Specialist Gloves | Blackbook
  • Specialist Gloves | Chocolate Chesterfield
  • Specialist Gloves | Cloud Chaster
  • Specialist Gloves | Lime Polycam
  • Specialist Gloves | Pillow Punchers
  • Specialist Gloves | Sunburst
  • Sport Gloves | Blaze
  • Sport Gloves | Creme Pinstripe
  • Sport Gloves | Frosty
  • Sport Gloves | Occult
  • Sport Gloves | Red Racer
  • Sport Gloves | Ultra Violet
  • Sport Gloves | Violet Beadwork

Valve change strategy surrounding skin monetization

The update means that there has been no new case since March 31, 2025, almost a year ago, when the Fever Case was introduced. Cases, which require a $2.49 USD key to open, have been the focus of the lawsuits targeting Valve for “illegal gambling.”

The suits, originating from the New York Attorney General Letitia James and Seattle-based law firm Hagens Berman, both cited the “near miss” mechanics featured in case openings as a similarity to slot machines.

Since last March, only Armory skins and Terminal skins have been added to the game. Armory skins can be redeemed by accumulating XP through the Armory Pass to earn stars, which you can redeem for skins.

Terminals are the newest way to get skins, with the first Terminal, entitled the ‘Sealed Genesis Terminal’, added to the game in September 2025.

Unlike cases, Terminals can be opened for free. The player is then offered a weapon and can choose whether to buy the weapon directly through Steam. After players decline five offers, the Terminal will destroy itself.

Crucially, the latest update has added Gold or ‘Rare Special Items’ to Terminals, allowing players to get gloves from Terminals for the first time.

The addition of these rare items to Terminals reflects Valve’s gradual shift away from cases in recent times. While Valve’s reasoning is not public, the scrutiny surrounding cases may be a significant motivator for the move.

Why do you think Valve is moving away from cases? You can discuss that, and more, on Insider Gaming’s Discord server!

For further information, you can read Valve’s public response to recent lawsuits here.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/counter-strike-2-dead-hand-collection/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Microsoft is Deleting All Traces of ‘This is an Xbox’ Marketing Campaign
item.content:encoded:
In 2024, Microsoft and Xbox launched a marketing campaign designed to indicate just how many things could be classified as an Xbox. The theme of the campaign was simply that – ‘This is an Xbox.’ It showed how everything from televisions to phones, and from consoles to VR headsets could be classed as an Xbox, but it unfortunately diluted the brand.

In recent hours, it has been noticed that Microsoft is silently shutting down all traces of the campaign, removing old news articles and blog posts that refer to ‘This is an Xbox’.

Maybe This Isn’t An Xbox After All

Eagle-eyed gamers have noticed that Microsoft has started shredding all traces of the This is an Xbox marketing campaign that surfaced in 2024. It was one of the first nods to Xbox being a platform and not just a home console device, but it didn’t go down well with fans and became something of a joke amongst the wider community.

People were lifting toasters and hair-dryers and declaring them as Xbox devices… You get the picture.

Following the shock appointment of Asha Sharma as the new leader of Xbox (and the retirement of Phil Spencer), the campaign headed by Sarah Bond has been silently stripped from the internet.

Everything related to This is an Xbox is now returning 404 errors. For instance, a page that would have once led you to everything related to that tag just returns:

It was recently claimed by some that the troublesome campaign had a hand in Sarah Bond’s sudden departure, which aligned with Phil Spencer’s retirement from Xbox.

However, despite the message having been entirely removed, it still stands somewhat. In a recent GDC presentation, the Xbox team revealed that the future of the console is a multi-platform thing. It still intends to offer a broad Xbox service across PC, handheld, console, VR, and everything else associated with the Xbox label.

That’s in addition to the recent announcement of Project Helix, the next-generation Xbox unit that’s being described as a hybrid PC-console invention.

Are you surprised to see the marketing campaign deleted by Microsoft? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Pokémon Pokopia has surged beyond two million sales

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1005)—Answers for Thursday, March 12
item.content:encoded:
How is it that another week is drawing to a close so quickly? We’re approaching the equinox, it feels like summer is on the horizon, and every day, another NYT Connections puzzle rises for us to dominate. That’s the theme of today, as you prepare to scroll down and pick up the best NYT Connections hints and tips in the business.

Read on to accomplish what you need today and get your day started the right way.

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (March 12)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • CUCKOO CLOCK
  • SWING
  • STEP
  • SANDBOX
  • HOURGLASS
  • MEXICAN FLAG
  • METRONOME
  • WEATHER VANE
  • WINDSHIELD WIPER
  • BUNKER
  • SPIN
  • PENDULUM
  • DESERT
  • REFORMER
  • FROOT LOOPS
  • BARRE

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Places or things within which you might find a bit of sand
  • Green – Things that have a consistent movement pattern
  • Blue – Exercise classes that typically involve a piece of equipment
  • Purple – Words that you might associate with a type of bird

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, March 12:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, March 12 (#1005)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – BUNKER, DESERT, HOURGLASS, SANDBOX
  • Green – METRONOME, PENDULUM, SWING, WINDSHIELD WIPER
  • Blue – BARRE, REFORMER, SPIN, STEP
  • Purple – CUCKOO CLOCK, FROOT LOOPS, MEXICAN FLAG, WEATHER VANE

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP
